Transcrição Completa: How to Crush Black Friday Cyber Monday with Nick Theriot
[00:00] Who here has started preparing for Black Friday by a show of hands and actually has a plan and everything ready to roll out to start executing on. Okay, great. People who didn't raise your hands, this will be exactly for you. Um, just a little heads up, I'm very very tactical with everything I do. So, if you watch my YouTube channel, it's same level of quality. Um, so it'll be really great game plan for all of your clients and stuff like that.
[00:30] I had my first Black Friday encounter in 2019 where I closed my first like ecom clients and we took this account from literally like negative on ads. They spent like 30k and made back 25k in revenue. And we came into their business, we helped them do 100k in the first 90 days, like a 3x return on ad spend.
[00:52] And that was my first ever Black Friday. At the time was spending roughly $500 a day making them back like $1,500 a day. And you know, for their first Black Friday, we made it back like 13, 14 grand in the weekend. Nothing crazy.
[01:08] But a lot of people that I was looking around, other mentors and things like that, they had all were talking about since like September 1st. Prepare for Black Friday. Prepare for Black Friday. And at the time, I'm like, what the fuck you got to prepare for? It's literally just waiting till Black Friday starts. Launch some ads to say 30% off and you're good to go.
[01:30] So I basically did that and my first Black Friday ever, you know, nothing crazy at the time. entry-level marketer. It felt great. So, next year over 2019 to 2020, I spent a lot of time preparation, talking to mentors and figuring out exactly what makes a great Black Friday.
[01:48] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "2020 Black Friday Results"
NÚMEROS: $69,000 em 2 dias, $13,000 em ad spend
VISUAL: Apresentação de resultados com destaque para valores em dólares, comparação de performance ano a ano
[01:52] And well, that same exact client, we often do roughly $69,000 in two days um with, let's see, $13,000 in ad spend. And I'll get I'll get why we only are able to do that in two days in a little bit. Um, but can anyone take a guess why we have to stop scaling?
[02:10] Okay, cool. And we'll get to that in a little bit, too, because that is a huge mistake that I've made is not having enough inventory to hit the numbers you want to hit.
[02:20] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "2021 Black Friday Results"
NÚMEROS: $734,000 em 7 dias, $83,000 em spend, 10x ROAS
VISUAL: Gráfico de crescimento exponencial, destaque para ROI, layout profissional com métricas principais
[02:22] Now, 2021 was my next Black Friday event and went a lot bigger and we did $734,000 in 7 days. Um, full transparency, there was a completely different brand. This brand was roughly doing, you know, 200 300k a month at the time when we came on board and we did that with roughly $83,000 in spend. So, just under a 10x return on ad spend.
[02:50] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "3 Core Parts of Black Friday Success"
NÚMEROS: 3 componentes principais, 10-14 dias de teasing
VISUAL: Três colunas distintas mostrando: List Building, Teasing (10-14 dias), Launch. Layout estruturado com ícones representando cada fase
[02:55] Now, what all goes into this? There's three core parts. The first part is list building, and that's something you're going to start doing now till Black Friday, Cyber Monday. Your list is your profit for Black Friday, Cyber Monday. The next thing is teasing, which we start roughly 10 to 14 days out from Black Friday, Cyber Monday. And that is teasing the event, getting people ready for the event. And then Black Friday, Cyber Monday launch.
[03:25] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "4 Things You Need to Prepare"
NÚMEROS: 4 elementos essenciais
VISUAL: Lista numerada de 1 a 4 com ícones: 1. Goal (alvo), 2. Offers (etiquetas de desconto), 3. Inventory (caixas de estoque), 4. Execution (engrenagens). Design limpo e organizado
[03:30] And there's four things that you need to prepare to make sure that you have a really amazing event. The first one is your goal. How much money do you want to make? have some clients ask that and they're like whatever's profitable. It's not really a clear goal there. We can spend 30 bucks, make them back 90 bucks and cool that's a good job well done on our side.
[03:50] Next thing is offers. What are the type of different offers you're going to offer? And I'm not just talking about the standard shit of 20% off, 30% off. Are you launching any new products, new product lines? Are you doing a discount? You doing a bonus gift? Items like that. That's very important right there. types of offers you should be running, not just during Black Friday, Cyber Monday, but Q4 in general.
[04:15] Next thing is inventory. Like I said with that first client, it's $69,000 in two days, and they ran out of inventory, and they couldn't scale Sunday and Monday. And lastly is execution, which will be probably roughly three parts of three quarters of this conver um this presentation, which is all the tactical side of the type of ads you need to run, campaign structures, and things like that.
[04:40] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Goal: How Much Money Do You Want to Make?"
NÚMEROS: Exemplos mencionados: $30 gasto → $90 retorno
VISUAL: Ícone de alvo com cifrão no centro, setas apontando para metas financeiras, layout motivacional
[04:42] So number one goal, how much money do you want to make in Q4? And again, this is very important right here because this is going to determine the inventory you need, the offers you need to run, and also the ad spend you need.
[05:00] Last year for one of that account, the account that did like $737,000 in 7 days, the problem we ran into was ad um spend limits. So we had to literally message Facebook in the middle of it, hey, we need more money. Like our daily spin limit was getting capped.
[05:18] So very vital to be able to have all that prepared now. So that way you can message Q um Facebook in advance to hey we need ad spend limit because we plan on spending this much as well as like credit cards, things like that, cash in the bank. Um so that's all important to be able to work backwards on.
[05:35] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Q4 Offers Strategy Timeline"
NÚMEROS: 25 de novembro (Black Friday), 28 de novembro (Cyber Monday), 29 de novembro (New Drop), 5 de dezembro (Christmas)
VISUAL: Timeline horizontal mostrando 4 períodos de ofertas: Black Friday/Cyber Monday (25-28 nov), New Drop (29 nov), Christmas (5 dez - Christmas), New Year (pós-Christmas). Código de cores diferente para cada período
[05:40] Next thing is offers. Now this will be a little bit different than you probably normally see. Um we split Q4 and Black Friday Cyber Monday to four core offers right here. So, Black Friday this year is on Friday, November 25th, and it's going to go all the way to Monday, November the 28th, uh, for Cyber Monday. And that's when we run our Black Friday, Cyber Monday.
[06:05] Um, we don't start any earlier than at least the Monday before Cyber Monday. Uh, we have some people that like to start November 1st for their Black Friday. And honestly, I don't really care for it too much. I it's quite frankly it's like you're just blowing your load early because you have a really amazing sales event starting off and then you see a dip mid November and then you see subpar results at a discount rate.
[06:30] So I like to catch the momentum of everyone else and launch it either that week or that Friday for our Black Friday Cyber event. Now Tuesday, November 29th, that is the day after Cyber Monday. One of the highest converting ways to get people to purchase from you who has already just purchased and spent a lot of money with you is offer a new line of products.
[06:55] So, hey, you just purchased from us. You spent a lot of money from us at a discounted rate. Um, thank you so much for your support this weekend. Here's a limited drop of new items that you've never seen before. And it recaptures that attention and gets them to come back in the store. And you're giving them at normal rate, no discounts or anything like that.
[07:15] Next thing is Christmas. So, we like to start our Christmas offer Monday, December 5th, and run it all the way up to Christmas. Um, this is your buy now, get before Christmas, and just your standard, hey, here's our Christmas offer. And then lastly is your New Year offer. Um, which we like to same way for Black Friday, Cyber Monday. We like to do a new drop right after Christmas. Best way to get people to convert again from you who just spent a lot of money with you.
[07:45] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Look at Previous Compelling Offers"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Ícone de lupa sobre dados históricos, gráficos de performance passada, análise de best sellers
[07:48] Now, you do want to look back at your previous compelling offers. So, look at previous sales events, previous, you know, items you've dropped and look for best sellers, high converting products, and um just other like, you know, offers and incentives that you've offered that work the best. Black Friday is not a time to test. It's figuring out what's worked well for you and increasing on that.
[08:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Bad Offer Example vs Good Offer"
NÚMEROS: $50 = 10% off, $100 = 15% off (exemplo ruim)
VISUAL: Comparação lado a lado: lado esquerdo mostra oferta complicada com múltiplos níveis de desconto (confusa, X vermelho); lado direito mostra oferta simples e direta (check verde)
[08:18] Next thing is the type of offers you run is very important. Okay? If you do something complicated like this, spend $50, get 10% off, spend a h 100red bucks, get 15% off. Who has seen stores like that where it's like this big long like list of different discounts and offers and stuff like that. It's too confusing. A confused mind will always say no.
[08:42] We like to be very direct with what we're offering. So, we just go straight with, hey, get a free bonus item for spending a hundred bucks or free shipping over $50 or, you know, just up to 20% off sitewide on the site, whatever the easiest offer is. And we only offer one.
[09:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "DO NOT Use Discount Codes"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Código de desconto com X vermelho grande, ícone de erro, alertas sobre problemas de digitação
[09:02] Next thing, do not use discount codes. That's a big thing that we've seen year-over-year where with Shopify, like people have trouble typing in the discount code. You know, sometimes you might put like a little space either before you type the code in or something like that and like it doesn't work. So then like now you have an angry customer in your comments. Hey, I tried the code. It didn't work.
[09:25] So we just recommend do not use discounts codes at all. We see a much higher conversion rate. It is a little bit more time consuming to mark down SKs and stuff like that. If you are on Shopify, there's an app called sales and discount manager that we use and you can literally just select collections, you know, the time period you want to discount it by and also show show down the markdown price so people can see the value that they're getting from that offer.
[09:52] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Good Offers Checklist"
NÚMEROS: 3 critérios principais
VISUAL: Lista com check marks: ✓ Simple, ✓ Automatically Discounted, ✓ Irresistible. Design limpo com ícones verdes de aprovação
[09:55] Um, and then obviously going into good offers, simple, automatically discounted and irresistible. Again, not a time to test new things. You want to focus on stuff that's previously worked best and be able to recreate that.
[10:10] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Inventory: The Biggest Mistake"
NÚMEROS: $69,000 em 2 dias (exemplo de falha por falta de inventário)
VISUAL: Ícone de caixas de estoque vazias, sinal de alerta vermelho, gráfico mostrando vendas interrompidas
[10:12] All right, inventory. This is the biggest one right here because again, going back to that client that showed you in the beginning, they never did this math. They just said, "Hey, I want to make a fuckload of money." And when we looked at their inventory, they didn't have it. And this is an early on learning mistake on my side because I didn't even ask that question myself. Do you have enough inventory to hit your goal?
[10:40] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Inventory Math Formula"
NÚMEROS: Goal: $200,000, Offer: 50% off, Needed: $400,000 retail value
VISUAL: Fórmula matemática clara: Revenue Goal ÷ Discount % = Required Inventory Value. Exemplo com $200k meta e 50% desconto = $400k necessário em inventário
[10:45] If you want to do a million dollars, right, cool. Do you have a million dollars worth as a retail value, need it, and inventory to hit that goal? And secondly, if you're offering a offer, so here's $200,000 is our goal. We want to do 50% off. Then we're going to need $400,000 of retail value to be able to hit that goal of $200,000. Anyone have any questions on that math? Does that look good? Okay, cool.
[11:15] Um, so yeah, so inventory is huge. Clients all the time come to us, hey Nick, I want to do a million dollars. Cool. Do you have enough inventory? Well, I got like 200k in inventory. We can do pre-orders the rest. No, that's terrible.
[11:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Best Sellers vs New Products Ratio"
NÚMEROS: 40% best sellers, 60% new products (exemplo ruim)
VISUAL: Gráfico de pizza mostrando distribuição inadequada de inventário, destaque em vermelho para o problema
[11:32] And then next thing, this is also a really big thing. So if we go back to that client that did 69,000 in two days, 40% of their inventory at the time was inventory that was best sellers and the other 60% was a whole line of new products that was completely different to anything else they offered and it performed horribly.
[11:55] So, when you're going into Black Friday, Cyber Monday, you want to make sure that it's you want to look at your best sellers and you want to make sure you have a good ample amount of inventory for best sellers. Um, that was clothing. So, there's like different SKs and stuff, sizes, colors. If you if your product is just one product with none of that, then great. That's amazing. It's less headache you have to involve. But that is a big thing that we've learned before.
[12:22] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "2-Day Performance Breakdown"
NÚMEROS: Dia 1: $24,000, Dia 2: $44,000, Total: $69,000
VISUAL: Gráfico de barras ascendente mostrando crescimento dia a dia, depois queda abrupta por falta de inventário
[12:25] Cool. And then again, just restating out that our best sellers basically ran out after two days. So, we were scaling up good. You can see, you know, the first day we did 24, second day 44,000. We could have kept going if we had the inventory. Um, but again, bestsellers ran off inventory. We had inventory of products that people didn't like and we had to start pulling back spend immediately to, you know, make sure we don't weren't burning money.
[12:55] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Execution: 4 Key Parts"
NÚMEROS: 4 componentes de execução
VISUAL: Diagrama com 4 blocos conectados: 1. List Building, 2. Teasing Offers, 3. Launch Structure, 4. Creatives. Fluxo direcional entre cada elemento
[13:00] All right, number four is execution. Um, this is where we're going to get more into the tactical side. Now that you did the planning and everything, um, budgeting, inventory, and all that, now is the execution. There's four key parts of execution. So, number one is the list building side. The bigger the list you have right now, the more money you can make Black Friday, Cyber Monday.
[13:22] Next thing is teasing offers where we're actually going to tease the event that's happening. So people are actually aware you're throwing this a really amazing event. The launch structure which I I'll be completely honest with you guys. I'm a chat at media buying for Facebook. So it's literally like one slide. Um and then the creatives you need literally give you the exact creatives you need to run for Black Friday Cyber Monday to where you can literally just give this to your copyriter, your designer and stuff like that and have them recreate it.
[13:52] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "List Building: 4 Categories"
NÚMEROS: 4 categorias principais
VISUAL: Quadro dividido em 4 seções: 1. Customer Acquisition (ícone de pessoa com +), 2. Facebook Groups (ícone de grupo), 3. Organic Followings (ícone de redes sociais), 4. SMS/Email Building (ícone de mensagem)
[13:55] So list building involves four different categories. Customer acquisition. Right now, you should be very hard on customer acquisition because your existing customers is what's going to make your revenue up for Black Friday, Cyber Monday. This is a great time for existing customers to come in, purchase more of your product, purchase gifts for relatives and stuff for Christmas and everything. It's a great gift um gifting time.
[14:22] So, you really want to hammer your customer acquisition. This is a time where you might as well, if you can afford it in your business, to accept a a slightly higher CPA with the intentions of monetizing those customers over Black Friday, Cyber Monday.
[14:38] Next thing, um, a lot of ecom brands now that we're working with are starting up Facebook groups with their customers and putting all their customers in a Facebook group. Um, this is a super amazing thing. By the way, uh my AVI and Redline stills where I originally got the idea from and it's really powerful to have one-on-one feedback with your customers because you can actively go ahead show them different products you're thinking about talk uh launching, get real life feedback and everything.
[15:08] And it's also a great place to immediately go ahead and just say, "Hey, offer live." So, putting more customers in your Facebook group. Some brands are trying to be like cool now and like use Discords and Telegram. So, like there's other alternatives as well. um just whatever fits your customer avatar the best.
[15:28] Next thing is organic followings. Um so even organic right now you'll notice in Q4 a lot of brands are offering like gift card giveaways in exchange for like followers and stuff like that. Um this is a great time to be doing that. I'm not completely like condoning that just because I think giving gift cards away for followers just doesn't really bring the best type of customer.
[15:50] But now is a great time to be hammering organic followings. Um, I'm getting all my brands right now to try to hammer Tik Tok right now organic because I do believe um, I have a buddy who's been like scaling stores organically to 5 10k a day on Tik Tok. And I think right now is a great time to be able to be hammering that as well.
[16:12] And then lastly, SMS and email building. So this again is be another part of your list that's going to make a lot of money for you Black Friday, Cyber Monday. And my favorite way right now to do it is just I'm sure all of y'all who have ecom store already have a little popup on your site to collect a discount code for entering email or free shipping for entering email.
[16:35] So now's the time where you want to make sure that conversion rate is the highest. So maybe offer something a little bit better now, but nothing too crazy that's going to throw off Black Friday, Cyber Monday.
[16:45] Go ahead.
[16:46] I've seen stores at 10%. It's probably the most common one right now. Um, I've also seen a lower conversion rate of like 5 to 8% by getting the SMS as well. So, like I make people hit like the little when the pops up, I make people type in their email, then hit continue, then it collects their SMS next.
[17:08] So, it's like a two-step optin. I see roughly at 5 to 8%, but the SMS I see way more profit from it. So, slightly lower conversion rate, but you're getting that SMS and email. And honestly guys, for Black Friday, Cyber Monday, do y'all ever see your inbox on email? It's fucked in there. So go for SMS as well. So that way you have a more direct, less competition on the phone.
[17:35] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Teasing Offers: The Fyre Festival Strategy"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Imagem do festival Fyre, comparação entre hype/expectativa vs realidade, analogia visual para estratégia de teasing
[17:38] All right. Teasing offers. Who has seen this movie right here? Great. Why did so many people go to that island and initially
[17:48] There you go. The tease was amazing to get everyone there and obviously unfortunately they got undelivered. So the same way fire was able to get everyone there, you need to do for your event.
[18:00] I see too many people just, all right, cool. We're going to, you know, back to like my first Black Friday, Cyber Monday, I just wait till the event, boom, launch some ads, and it didn't really pop up that much. But when you do a correct tease, you build the momentum and you have people literally sitting on the edge of their seats waiting for the site to go live. And that's what we want.
[18:22] We look at Black Friday, Cyber Monday as one huge party. And we want to promote that party. And the art of a successful Black Friday, Cyber Monday is in the TEAS.
[18:35] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Day 1 Launch Results"
NÚMEROS: €46,000 em 1 hora
VISUAL: Gráfico de spike dramático mostrando €46k de receita concentrada na primeira hora, destaque visual para o impacto do teasing adequado
[18:38] And this is just to show you right here um for our day one launch for that one client uh we roughly did €46,000 in literally one hour um by simply teasing up properly. And I'm going to go exactly how to tease correctly because if you do it correct incorrect, it's not a great time.
[19:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Teasing Timeline & Performance Dip"
NÚMEROS: 14 dias antes, queda de performance em 10-11 de novembro
VISUAL: Timeline mostrando 14 dias de preparação, gráfico com dip de performance mid-novembro, explicação do comportamento do consumidor
[19:02] So we start this out 14 days before Black Friday um Black Friday and Cyber Monday. And here's also a great thing as well. During that 14 days before Black Friday, Cyber Monday, we see a big drop in performance. Anyone else see that as well? Consumers start to pull back money because Black Friday is coming up. They're saving their money and everything. And usually we start seeing that roughly like November 10th, 11th, we see a dip in performance.
[19:30] That's okay because we're launching Facebook lead form campaigns. And just kind of essentially like the hydra strategy, we're hammering people on lead forms and we're acquiring SMS and email and we're telling people about the events and we're hyping people up for the event and we're getting them to opt in.
[19:50] And also too, um, we do like to create landing pages for clients as well. And literally the landing page is as simple as headline, optin box, and a counter and telling people about the offer. And we'll use this so that way we can email our existing customers um to get them on a VIP list inside of Claio.
[20:12] Uh we'll, you know, if we're working with any influencers, I have one client that's working with a lot of influencers this year. Um and then also your social followings, organic, Tik Tok, Instagram, all that good stuff right there.
[20:25] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Klaviyo VIP List Strategy"
NÚMEROS: Lista: "Black Friday Cyber Monday 2022 VIP"
VISUAL: Funil mostrando múltiplas fontes (lead forms, landing page, influencers, social) convergindo para uma lista única no Klaviyo
[20:28] Um and again, we're going to direct both the lead forms and the landing page um inside of a Clayo list called Black Friday, Cyber Monday 2022 VIP. And we're just going to fatten this list up. We had 14 days to fatten it up as much people as possible.
[20:45] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Lead Generation Results 2020"
NÚMEROS: $5,000 spend, 5,000 leads, $1 por lead
VISUAL: Métricas de performance destacadas, CPL de $1, total de leads adquiridos, comparação custo-benefício
[20:48] Um so here's just to show you one of our accounts. Uh $5,000 spend. This was on the um the 2020 Black Friday. We did $69,000 in two days. Um we spent $5,000 and required 5,000 leads. Literally a dollar per lead. Uh and it pisses me off because honestly we could have spent a lot more now that I've learned how to actually see what's a good value KPI per lead. And I'll show you that in a second of how to actually judge that.
[21:18] Um, that's literally using all warm traffic plus broad uh broad targeting.
[21:25] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "VIP List Lead Form Creative Example"
NÚMEROS: Até 65% off, 24 horas de acesso antecipado
VISUAL: Exemplo de anúncio de lead form: texto "Sign up to VIP list", destaque para "up to 65% off", badge de "24-hour early access", design clean e direto
[21:28] And this is to show you the actual creative we used right here. Um, again, sign up to our VIP list, get access to deals up to 65% off. I'm showing people what that they'll be getting. And I'm also calling it a VIP list. People like status. So, let's give them a status by opting into this list. And I'm also offering 24-hour early access. Why would you want 24-hour early access to a cra very crazy period of time of the year just for you?
[21:58] Huh?
[22:00] Get what I want.
[22:01] Exactly. You're first in line. You don't have to worry about fighting everyone else for inventory. You don't have to worry about your favorite colors and your sizes being, you know, out. And basically, that's just simple creative. Um, and then also to contact information. Like I said, just email and phone number. Very simple.
[22:22] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Early Access Strategy Details"
NÚMEROS: 24 horas de acesso antecipado (testando 1 hora também)
VISUAL: Comparação de janelas de acesso, gráfico mostrando rush de vendas concentrado vs distribuído
[22:25] We like to offer access up to 24 hours early. Um, I've haven't tried anything earlier than that. I find 24 hours is really good because honestly, you just get one really amazing rush for 1 hour and then it starts to dip out the rest of the day. Um, so I do have some clients that are experimenting with one hour this year. So that's also another thing too you can try out as well.
[22:50] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Urgency Tactics: Password Lock + Limited Gifts"
NÚMEROS: Primeiros 100 compradores, 4,000 pessoas na lista, 1,000 itens em estoque
VISUAL: Ícone de cadeado no site Shopify, contador de "First 100", comparação visual 4,000 pessoas vs 1,000 produtos (escassez)
[22:52] and we'll literally take their Shopify site, passcode lock the whole site, and we'll blast out the code. So, it's literally a private shopping hour just for you. And there's a few other criteria we like to do here. So, first 100 people get X. We found that with the intentions of getting as many people in the edge of their seats to literally type in that code, another really powerful maneuver we like to promote is that the first 100 people get X.
[23:20] This might be a free gift. This might be a extra percent off. Us personally, we found the free gift to work the best. Hey guys, we're running we're making a limited edition t-shirt that we're never going to supply again. And the first 100 people who get this who purchase on Black Friday. I'm not saying we're going to pull it. It's not a drawing or a winner thing like that. It's the first 100 people who purchase.
[23:45] So, if I'm telling 4,000 people that the first hundred people will get a free t-shirt, would that increase the urgency to be number one on that list or number one to the website to purchase?
[23:58] Next thing is I like to call out I'll literally put it this in an ad and I'll put this on an email as well. Hey, there's over 4,000 people of you who subscribe to this list, but unfortunately we only have a thousand items in inventory. You need to be there quick. you need to be there first if you want to buy something. Again, increasing that urgency.
[24:20] It's very important that we're leveraging all of these psychological um you know, triggers as like to call them. So that way we try to get as many people ready and want to be number one on that site when we go live. And that's how you're able to get that big rush of people.
[24:38] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Additional Teasing Creative Examples"
NÚMEROS: 5,000 pessoas inscritas, 4,000 itens em inventário
VISUAL: Múltiplos exemplos de anúncios: 1) Vídeo com founder e Lamborghini Aventador ao fundo, 2) Behind-the-scenes do processo de criação, 3) Contagem regressiva com números de lista vs inventário
[24:40] And this is show you some more um kind of ads we've ran during the teasing stage as well. Um, so this is another version of the sign up to our VIP list right here. Um, this particular client is a clothing brand and she was very um, you know, very upfront. All the all of her customers know who she is and stuff like that. So, she filmed a video right here and she was also flexing her husband's aventador behind it. Just can't really see.
[25:08] Um, another one that we ran to that same hype up list and everything right there was showing off a little bit of the creation process and showing off some of the items that be dropping on Black Friday, Cyber Monday. Again, just kind of dangling the carrot in front of people, getting people ready, getting people hyped up.
[25:28] And then the last one right here is basically showcasing again how many people subscribed. So, we had 5,000 people that subscribed on the list. there's only 4,000 items in inventory and you need to act quickly and you know let's see yeah so that'll be good and also just re reinforcing the fact that a site will be locked just for the VIP list. Cool.
[25:52] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "How to Calculate Acceptable CPL (Cost Per Lead)"
NÚMEROS: Fórmula: (90-day email revenue ÷ total emails) ÷ target ROAS, Exemplo: $3.32 CPL para 4x ROAS
VISUAL: Fórmula matemática destacada em box, exemplo de cálculo passo a passo, referência ao Klaviyo para dados, target ROAS de 4x
[25:55] So here's some tactical stuff to figure out what's an acceptable CPL. So the biggest challenge with building up a list and everything like that is you have no fucking clue what's an acceptable CPL. Like I had one client do a $5 CPL last year. another client that did a dollar, $3. What is an acceptable cost per lead that you can actually scale up on? That's the biggest question.
[26:20] So, the biggest thing I found that works the best way to figure this out, take your total email revenue from 90 days on Claio and then report it specifically from Clavio. So, not your overall sales, your email reported revenue, and divided by your total emails. I'll just go in Clavio, create a segment, and I'll do um like everyone who's subscribed, everyone who's unsubscribed, and all customers to get the total amount of emails I had during that period of time.
[26:48] And I'll divide it by my target rorowass, which is four. So, do all that math right there. And that puts me at a $332 cost per lead um to be able to do a 4x. So, when I spend a dollar cost per lead, what does that tell me? could have spent a lot more. Could have spent a lot more and you know that would have made a bigger first day launch.
[27:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "CPL Management Strategy"
NÚMEROS: 20% ajustes diários, 50-100% para scaling agressivo
VISUAL: Flowchart de decisão: Se atingindo target → aumentar 20% spend; Se não atingindo → diminuir 20% spend; Para scaling agressivo → 50-100% aumentos
[27:18] So, and the way I basically managed that from a media buyer perspective, if we're hitting our target, cool, increase spend by 20% daily. Um, if we're not hitting our target, okay, cool. Decrease spend by 20%. And depending on where we're at in relation to the spin on that brand, I might even do 50% to 100% spin if I need to spend more quick. And obviously, if you're not hitting that, that's something to do with your creative performance, uh, messaging, or even your offer.
[27:48] And then same thing is you don't want to forget to leverage these tactics on email, organic, and influencers. Email, be emailing your other lists that are not people on your Black Friday, Cyber Monday list. Email them. Let them know about the big event. start showing off some of the products and stuff like that and keep directing them to that landing page to subscribe to the list and same thing for organic. Make some organic posts about it.
[28:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Alphalete Teasing Strategy Case Study"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Timeline de estratégia da Alphalete: 1) Sneak peeks de produtos em desenvolvimento, 2) Influencers fazendo try-on hauls, 3) Fotografia profissional 24-48h antes, 4) Anúncio do evento
[28:18] Um I know this brand called Alfleet. Who's heard of Alphalite before? Okay, cool. Literally Alfleet, this is their teasing structure. They number one, they start showing off some glimpses of like the products that are like in like this like this drafting phase, I guess you could say. Then they start showing off some of the influencers. The influencers will all create a video Alfleet try on hall, you know, and at the end of the tryhon hall, they tell them, "Hey, you're going to get it on this date." Okay.
[28:48] Um, and then obviously 24 hours out and 48 hours out, they start showing off some of like the more high speed like photography and stuff like that. Um, and then announcing the big event. So that's how they're leveraging influencers and organic as well. Um, and again, you're all directing people to that one Clavio list to fatten that list up as much as possible because that's where you're going to make bulk of your profits and your revenue for Black Friday.
[29:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Media Buying Structure: The Chad Approach"
NÚMEROS: 1 CBO campaign, 4 adsets, 4 tipos de anúncios
VISUAL: Estrutura simples de campanha: 1 CBO no topo, ramificando para 4 adsets (Broad, Social Engagement 180d, Website Visitors, Email/Purchase Lists), cada um recebendo os mesmos 4 ads
[29:18] All right. Like I said, I'm a complete Chad when it comes to media buying. Um, I'm literally for Black Friday, Cyber Monday, I'll take one CBO campaign. I'll create four different types of ads and I'm literally just tossing them into these four adsets right here. Broad targeting. Um, be complete frank. I haven't ran a single retargeting audience all year long and my clients made 10x more money with Facebook ads.
[29:45] Um, all I do is broad targeting. I personally think it retargets people as well for you. So, I don't even do retargeting, but I'm going to do it just for Black Friday, Cyber Monday because I'm just trying to make sure I spend as much as possible and also give Facebook like some little bit of audiences I want to target.
[30:05] Uh, so I'm doing broad targeting, social engagement, 180 days. This is all your Facebook and Instagram engagement audiences. Uh, website visitors. I'm also including view content, add to cart, initiate checkout, all of your other events for that particular adset. Um then adset four, all of your email list, your Black Friday Cyber Monday list, your um purchase list, your just regular email list, and your purchase 180 days and you'll you'll deploy those four ads in each of those adsets and that'll start you off for day one.
[30:38] And then obviously there's some few other things I'll mention too um going into it.
[30:42] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Budget Strategy Formula"
NÚMEROS: Exemplo: $200,000 goal ÷ 5x ROAS = $40,000 spend ÷ 4 dias = $10,000/dia
VISUAL: Fórmula de cálculo de budget diário, breakdown matemático claro, destaque para começar alto e reduzir vs tentar escalar
[30:45] Now, the next thing is how much do you need to spend for Black Friday Cyber Monday. So again, if your revenue is $200,000, that's your goal for Black Friday, Cyber Monday, and you want to do that at a 5x return on adsman, then you need to spend at least $40,000 over those four days. So if I got to spend $40,000 over four days, I'm going to start my daily budget on that CBO at $10,000 a day.
[31:12] I found after running three different Black Friday or Cyber Mondays, I found it's significantly easier to start at a high budget and pull down then try to scale up on Black Friday, Cyber Monday. It's a lot easier to pull um scale back than to try to increase spend on Black Friday, Cyber Monday. So, I like to start big and then pull budget back.
[31:35] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Daily Management Schedule"
NÚMEROS: 4 check-ins por dia: 8 AM, 12 PM, 4 PM, 8 PM
VISUAL: Timeline diária com 4 pontos de verificação marcados, ícones de relógio, processo de análise Shopify revenue vs ad spend
[31:38] And I'll look at the ad account literally four times a day when I'm spending this. I'm looking at 8:00, 12:00, 4:00, and 8 o'clock again at night. And all I'm doing is measuring total Shopify revenue versus total ad spend. And if I'm hitting my KPI, if 5x rows, that that's the goal, I'm increasing spend by 20%. Some accounts and they're doing much better than established KPI, I'll increase 50% to 100%. Go for it.
[32:05] Are you increasing that up to four times per day?
[32:08] Yeah, absolutely. Black Friday, Cyber Monday, that's the only time you can go hard with increasing. Um, I've also seen duplicating the campaign. So, instead of going like say for example 8 o'clock in the morning, we're already crushing it. We're doing like a 10x ro day. I'm like, "All right, we need to spend more." I might just duplicate the campaign, go 20k a day like that.
[32:32] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "4 Creative Types for Black Friday"
NÚMEROS: 4 tipos de criativos
VISUAL: Grid 2x2 mostrando: 1) Best performing ads com banner BFCM, 2) Solid graphics com texto, 3) Animações 2D curtas, 4) Photo/video shoots específicos de BFCM
[32:35] All right, creatives. These are the four creatives that we found that work the best on Black Friday, Cyber Monday. Take your best performing ad creatives right now, your top three in your account, and put a Black Friday, Cyber Monday banner on it with the promo code that you're going to run or um this like the the total percent off you're going to be running.
[32:58] Um solid graphics with text. So, just making like a solid little image ad. Um just graphics with a big text that says Black Friday 60% off sale. Uh short little 2D animations. You can take your solid graphics ad and just animate it. uh little gifts and stuff like that and also specific photooots, video shoots specific to Black Friday, Cyber Monday.
[33:20] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Creative Example 1: Solid Graphics"
NÚMEROS: Até 60% off
VISUAL: Exemplo de anúncio com gráficos sólidos, texto grande "Black Friday up to 60% off", design on-brand, fundo limpo
[33:22] And just to show you a few of those what those look like. Um so this is a image ad right here. Again, just solid solid um graphics ad with text. Black Friday up to 60% off discount. And I'm also making sure that it's on brand so people recognize it.
[33:40] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Market Awareness Level: Aware"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Referência ao livro "Breakthrough Advertising", conceito de "market awareness", destaque para simplicidade vs elaboração
[33:42] And we're also dealing with a market awareness level of um aware. aware is literally the easiest level of market awareness. If any you guys read Breakthrough Advertising, it's one of my favorite book ever to write and that's what we're doing in the ad. We're literally just stating that we have a Black Friday sale and it's live and here's the discount you're getting.
[34:05] Don't try to be elaborate on Black Friday because right now you want to be very easy for people to see because of how many ads they're getting hit with. If they have a long ad to read, they're not going to read that shit They're going to keep scrolling.
[34:20] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Creative Example 2: UGC Ad"
NÚMEROS: 60% off
VISUAL: Exemplo de UGC creator experimentando diferentes calças, banner de "Black Friday 60% off" no final do vídeo
[34:22] Um, and then the second ad right here, this was actually a UGC ad. Literally was just a UGC creator trying on the different types of pants right there. And at the end, we put Black Friday 60% off.
[34:35] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Creative Example 3: Best Performing Ad Modified"
NÚMEROS: 40% off sitewide
VISUAL: Exemplo de placa de endereço de metal (best performer invencível), banner adicionado: "Black Friday Sale Now Live 40% off sitewide"
[34:38] Here's an example right here. Um, the little metal address sign one where we took our best performing ad. Um, this this blows my mind. This one specific creative. We've tried hundreds of creatives against it. We cannot beat it at all. Um, we literally took this ad right here. It just added a banner. Black Friday sale now live 40% off sitewide. again text very straightforward.
[35:02] And then uh same thing for the icon one that was a best performing ad and we just put a discount um discount and Black Friday on it.
[35:12] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Schedule Ads in Advance Warning"
NÚMEROS: 1 semana de antecedência recomendada
VISUAL: Ícone de erro do Facebook, alerta de servidor, calendário mostrando agendamento antecipado, aviso sobre sobrecarga de sistema
[35:15] Who here before Black Friday, Cyber Monday tried launching ads Thursday night and got the little server error on Facebook, fucking not loading, campaigns not creating. You want to make sure you start taking action on all of this like now so that way you can actually launch your ads up to a week before Black Friday, Cyber Monday. schedule everything out in the ads manager because there's too many times that we've tried launching an ad and Facebook's just randomly not working for whatever reason. There's literally everyone who runs ads at once is trying to publish shit and it's not working. So that's also another big little tip right there just to avoid those issues.
[35:52] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Complete Creative Rounds Needed"
NÚMEROS: 6 rounds de criativos
VISUAL: Checklist completa: 1) Teasing the Optin, 2) Countdown Before Launch (72h), 3) Black Friday Launch Ads, 4) Weekend Refresh, 5) Cyber Monday Refresh, 6) Post-Black Friday Launch, 7) Holiday Offer
[35:55] All right, creative you need. This is all this rounds of creative you'll need. Um this is what like me and my team are already taking action on and preparing now for all of our clients teasing the optin. So, all the creatives that we went over to actually tease in to get people ready for the event.
[36:12] Um, countdown before launch. So, again, I like to launch some ads roughly 72 hours before the launch of the event. Let them know that, hey, there's 72 hours until the big event starts. Here's a reminder of everything that's awesome that's going to happen, everything that's going to be on sale, the big discount, and there's over 4,000 people on this list, and we only have 1,000 items in inventory. So, we want to count did that countdown ad and then obviously your Black Friday launch ads that day.
[36:42] Second thing is a weekend refresh. So, I've seen it before where we launched ads on Friday, didn't really see the performance we wanted and then we took a look, we tested some new creatives and the new creatives took off. So, you want to have some extra ads prepared in case your initial round because this last thing you want to do is stress out your designer and stuff like that because hey, Black Friday ads didn't work and I know it's in the weekend. can you help me create me some new ads? And then you're missing out on time that you could be spending money.
[37:12] Next thing is Cyber Monday. So, we like to do a Cyber Monday refresh. Now, look, if your Black Friday ads are still working amazing going into Monday, I would still launch the the Monday ads, but nine times out of 10, our Black Friday ads just keep spinning throughout the week, throughout Monday, and we see amazing performance. So, if you do have some issues though, then launch some some new stuff on Cyber Monday as well, just to help out with performance.
[37:38] And then post Black Friday launch. If you're going to launch an offer right after Black Friday on that Tuesday, then that's a great time for you to do your post Black Friday launch um and whatever ads you need for that specific product. And then obviously your holiday offer which will launch the following Monday and that'll be like your you know Christmas and um you know Christmas sale things like that.
[38:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Don't Forget Website Banners"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Exemplo de website com tema de Black Friday completo vs apenas banner simples no topo, comparação de conversão
[38:02] Um and also too don't forget about website banners. I see a lot of people that's literally so focused on the ad side that they never create anything for the website. And we always see a higher converting website when it's themed Black Friday than just driving people to a website that literally just has like maybe a little banner at the top says like Black Friday 20% off. So make sure you focus on your website banners.
[38:28] Um and then if you're doing any organic content plus emails that you need for um the email content and then influencers. So that's another c creative demanding area.
[38:40] All right. And that is it.
[38:42] Good. Thank you.
📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO
NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:
2019 (Primeiro BFCM): $30k spend → $25k revenue (negativo) → $100k nos primeiros 90 dias com 3x ROAS → $13-14k no weekend de Black Friday
2020: $69,000 em 2 dias com $13,000 em ad spend
2021: $734,000 em 7 dias com $83,000 em spend (quase 10x ROAS)
Day 1 Launch 2021: €46,000 em 1 hora
Lead Generation: $5,000 spend = 5,000 leads ($1 CPL)
CPL Aceitável Calculado: $3.32 para 4x ROAS target
Gestão de Spend: Aumentos de 20-100% diários dependendo de performance
Budget Diário Inicial: $10,000/dia para meta de $200k
Check-ins: 4x por dia (8 AM, 12 PM, 4 PM, 8 PM)
ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:
Timeline Q4:
Black Friday/Cyber Monday: 25-28 de novembro
New Product Drop: 29 de novembro (dia seguinte ao Cyber Monday)
Christmas Offer: 5 de dezembro até o Natal
New Year Drop: Pós-Natal
3 Fases Principais:
List Building (agora até BFCM) - "Sua lista é seu lucro"
Teasing (10-14 dias antes) - Construir momentum
Launch (4 dias do evento)
List Building (4 Categorias):
Customer Acquisition (aceitar CPA mais alto para monetizar no BFCM)
Facebook Groups (feedback direto + anúncio imediato)
Organic Followings (TikTok orgânico 5-10k/dia)
SMS/Email Building (popup 2-step: email → SMS, 5-8% conversion)
Teasing Strategy:
14 dias antes: Facebook Lead Forms + Landing Pages
Criar lista "BFCM 2022 VIP" no Klaviyo
Oferecer 24h de acesso antecipado (testando 1h)
Site com password lock para VIPs
Primeiros 100 compradores ganham gift exclusivo
Comunicar: X pessoas na lista vs Y itens em estoque
Media Buying Structure:
1 CBO campaign
4 Adsets: Broad / Social Engagement 180d / Website Visitors / Email Lists
4 tipos de ads em cada adset
Começar com budget alto e reduzir (não tentar escalar durante BFCM)
ESTRUTURA:
4 Pilares de Preparação:
Goal - Meta financeira clara (não "o que for lucrativo")
Offers - Simples, automáticas, irresistíveis
Inventory - Matemática: Goal ÷ Discount % = Inventory Needed
Execution - Táticas específicas
Regras de Ofertas:
NUNCA usar discount codes (problemas de digitação)
UMA oferta apenas (não múltiplos tiers)
Automaticamente aplicado no checkout
Focar em best sellers comprovados (não testar)
App recomendado: "Sales and Discount Manager" (Shopify)
Tipos de Ofertas Válidas:
Free gift com compra mínima
Free shipping sobre valor X
% off sitewide
New product launches
Inventory Planning:
40% best sellers vs 60% produtos novos = ERRO
Focar em best sellers comprovados
Calcular: Se meta $200k com 50% off → precisa $400k em retail value
Preparar ad spend limits com Facebook antecipadamente
4 Tipos de Criativos:
Best performers atuais + banner BFCM
Solid graphics com texto grande
Animações 2D curtas (GIFs)
Photo/video shoots específicos BFCM
Rounds de Criativos Necessários:
Teasing the Optin (14 dias antes)
Countdown Before Launch (72h antes)
Black Friday Launch Ads
Weekend Refresh (backup se ads não performarem)
Cyber Monday Refresh
Post-Black Friday Launch (Tuesday new drop)
Holiday Offer (Christmas)
Fórmula CPL Aceitável:
(90-day email revenue ÷ total emails) ÷ target ROAS = CPL máximo aceitável
Gestão de Budget:
Hitting KPI → aumentar 20% diariamente
Crushing KPI → aumentar 50-100%
Not hitting → reduzir 20%
Opção: duplicar campanha inteira se performando muito bem
Market Awareness:
Usar nível "Aware" (Breakthrough Advertising)
Mensagens simples e diretas
Não elaborar muito (muitos ads competindo por atenção)
DADOS FINANCEIROS:
Histórico de Performance:
2019: Negativo → 3x ROAS → $13-14k BFCM weekend
2020: $69k revenue / $13k spend = 5.3x ROAS (limitado por inventário)
Dia 1: $24k
Dia 2: $44k
Parou por falta de best sellers
2021: $734k revenue / $83k spend = 8.8x ROAS
€46k na primeira hora
Cliente fazendo $200-300k/mês antes
Lead Generation ROI:
$1 CPL alcançado
$3.32 CPL seria aceitável para 4x ROAS
5,000 leads com $5,000 spend
Potencial de ter gasto muito mais
Exemplo de Budget Planning:
Meta: $200,000 revenue
Target ROAS: 5x
Spend necessário: $40,000
Dias: 4
Budget diário inicial: $10,000
Problemas Financeiros Identificados:
Ad spend limits do Facebook (precisar pedir aumento mid-event)
Falta de inventário = oportunidade perdida
60% de inventário em produtos não-testados = desperdício
Não calcular CPL aceitável = deixar dinheiro na mesa
DURAÇÃO: ~39 minutos
FORMATO: Apresentação tática de estratégia Black Friday/Cyber Monday com slides, exemplos de anúncios e case studies
